rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE D.F. & A.J. RAVETTI FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's motion for summary judgment is denied. Respondent filed a claim before the applicable statute of limitations in CCP section 366.2 had run. PC section 19253(b), therefore, tolls the statute of limitations until the trustee has rejected the claim, which she has not done. Nothing in section 19253(b) requires that the trustee have commenced the optional statutory process of limiting the time to file a claim, let alone that the trustee, having initiated the process by filing a notice, have perfected the section 19100 limitation on filing claims. Even assuming that the trustee's failure to publish notice would not have limited a creditor's time to make a claim, such that any claim filed after February 19, 2014 would have been barred because the 366.2 limitation period would already have run, nothing in the statutory scheme suggests that a claim filed within the limitations period would nonetheless be invalidated by virtue of the trustee's actions. Furthermore, the claimant is not required to treat the trustee's silence as a rejection of the claim that would trigger an end to the tolling period. Nor is the trustee under any statutory duty to accept or reject the claim within a specific time period. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise opposing counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, July 26, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE D.F. & A.J. RAVETTI FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's motion is denied. Respondent has now made discovery. The Court is not satisfied that the parties engaged in sufficient reasonable good faith efforts to avoid bringing this discovery dispute to court, and both sides share responsibility for that. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise opposing counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, July 26, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE D.F. & A.J. RAVETTI FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
rulings.Rulings: Respondent's motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Court determines that Respondent's claim is not barred by the statute of limitations. Respondent filed a claim before the applicable statute of limitations in CCP section 366.2 had run. PC section 19253(b), therefore, tolls the statute of limitations until the trustee has rejected the claim, which she has not done. Nothing in section 19253(b) requires that the trustee have commenced the optional statutory process of limiting the time to file a claim, let alone that the trustee, having initiated the process by filing a notice, have perfected the section 19100 limitation on filing claims. Even assuming that the trustee's failure to publish notice would not have limited a creditor's time to make a claim, such that any claim filed after February 19, 2014 would have been barred because the 366.2 limitation period would already have run, nothing in the statutory scheme suggests that a claim filed within the limitations period would nonetheless be invalidated by virtue of the trustee's actions. Furthermore, the claimant is not required to treat the trustee's silence as a rejection of the claim that would trigger an end to the tolling period. Nor is the trustee under any statutory duty to accept or reject the claim within a specific time period. The Court denies the portion of the motion dealing with laches. The Court cannot determine on this record that there is no role for laches either in determining the arguably equitable issues whether Respondent's arguable delay in addressing the claim as trustee may have violated her duties as trustee and affected Petitioner's ability as real party in interest to defend against Respondent's claim or in determining whether Respondent as trustee should be instructed in some manner as to her response to the claim. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise opposing counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, July 26, 2016.
